title: manihot tristis subsp. tristis
intro: Manihot tristis subsp. tristis is a subspecies of the Manihot tristis plant, commonly known as a type of cassava or tapioca. It is a perennial shrub native to Brazil and is used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Manihot tristis subsp. tristis plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
